rulings.Case Title: SAMIR HINN ET AL VS. DIANA TOM SHARE TRUST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 21, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO TAX COSTS is continued to March 5, 2024. The Honorable Charles F. Haines recuses himself. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PARASTOO EMAMI VS. SCOTT MORRIS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 21, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT LESLIE MACKAY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, in part and OVERRULED, in part. Defendant's Demurrer to the Second Cause of Action for Fraudulent Concealment is OVERRULED. Defendant has 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an answer. Defendant's Demurrer to the Fourth Cause of Action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress is SUSTAINED. No opposition. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: SARAH LEWIS VS. KENNETH RUNYON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 21, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANTS ENA REALES AND LUIS MAGALLON'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED as follows: Defendant's request to strike specified general damages is GRANTED. Under CCP Section 425.10(a)(2), an amount demanded shall not be stated in an action for personal injury, so the language "of $250,000" in Plaintiff's prayer for relief is hereby stricken. Defendant's request to strike punitive damages is GRANTED with leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to state sufficient facts as to Defendant Reales and Magallon to support punitive damages. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
